title: Hierarchy of Open Sets in a Metric Space
description: A visual representation showing how open sets are defined by open balls and how they form the building blocks for topology in a metric space.
relationships: d is the metric defining distance
An Open Ball B(x, ε) is the set of all points within distance ε from x
A set U is Open if for every point x in U, there exists an ε > 0 such that B(x, ε) is entirely contained in U
The union of any collection of open sets is open
The finite intersection of open sets is open
